mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 6.5 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, causing equity markets to price in lower corporate earnings and produce negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer demand contraction, forcing firms to cut capital expenditures and leading to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST yield positive forward returns in more than 60 percent of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding two percent annualized signals robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization, driving short-term positive equity momentum through improved corporate revenue expectations.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth above two percent correlates with negative five-day returns in more than fifty percent of observations, or Rank IC falls below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index growth exceeding two percent annualized while policy rates remain near zero creates negative real yields, prompting investors to rotate out of equities into inflation-protected assets and depressing forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in more than sixty percent of periods when CPIAUCSL exceeds two percent, or the predictive alpha decays to zero within three months.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid nonfarm payroll growth combined with a falling unemployment rate indicates extreme labor market tightness that compresses corporate profit margins through wage inflation, resulting in negative equity forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE produces positive forward returns in more than fifty-five percent of instances, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly gross domestic product growth above two point five percent annualized confirms sustained macroeconomic expansion, supporting corporate earnings trajectories and generating positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above two point five percent fails to predict positive returns, with Rank IC remaining below zero over consecutive quarterly evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal manufacturing contraction and reduced economic activity, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Consecutive INDPRO declines are followed by positive five-day returns in more than sixty percent of cases, or the signal shows no predictive power in walk-forward validation.
